expect: Historian repair reduces 32 stale to under 25 via automated domain routing. history_integrity periodic baseline advances from 38% S429.
actual: Historian repair shows 34 stale items (1 maintenance, 9 frontiers, 24 domains). 24/24 domain items are never-visited domains (stale=457s) — not fixable by historian work, require dispatch. history_integrity: commit 100%, attribution 100%, experiment outcomes 57% (S400+ only, 32.8% overall), lane history preserved. Paper reswarm v0.25.3→v0.26.0 completed (13 stale drift points fixed). 32→34 stale (concurrent session added 2 items).
diff: NEGATIVE: stale count increased 32→34 rather than decreasing to <25. Root cause: 24/34 stale items are domain frontiers that have NEVER had a DOMEX lane — historian repair cannot fix dispatch-absent domains, only dispatch_optimizer can. The 10 actionable items (1 maintenance + 9 frontier) require specific domain work, not routing. history_integrity outcome rate advanced from 38% baseline (S429) to 57% (S457) via natural experiment accumulation, not this session's work. The expectation was miscalibrated: historian role does not have authority to create domain lanes. Correct framing: historian diagnoses, dispatch cures.
lesson_candidate: Historian repair tool conflates two failure classes: (1) never-visited domains needing first dispatch (structural gap), (2) genuinely stale items with recent evidence not yet incorporated (historian gap). 24/34 items are class 1, only 10 are class 2. The tool should distinguish these classes to avoid inflating the stale count with items the historian role cannot fix.
